task_completion.description: Agent successfully logs into the Rancher dashboard.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Rancher UI dashboard is visible, indicating the agent has logged in using the provided credentials.

task_completion.description: The Bitnami Helm repository is added.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Bitnami repository (https://charts.bitnami.com/bitnami) is visible in Rancher's Repositories/Apps section, or terminal output shows successful 'helm repo add'.

task_completion.description: The 'nginx' chart is deployed with the release name 'corp-proxy' in the 'dmz' namespace.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Rancher UI or kubectl terminal output shows an application or deployment named 'corp-proxy' located specifically in the 'dmz' namespace.

task_completion.description: The proxy successfully starts and is running, indicating the agent fixed the intentional ImagePullBackOff issue in the provided values file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The pods for the 'corp-proxy' deployment are shown in a 'Running' or 'Active' state. There should be no 'ImagePullBackOff' or 'ErrImagePull' errors visible for this deployment.

task_completion.description: The deployment runs with exactly 2 replicas.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Rancher UI or kubectl output shows '2/2' ready pods or explicitly lists 2 replicas for the 'corp-proxy' deployment.

task_completion.description: The service exposes HTTP on NodePort 30080.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The service details for 'corp-proxy' in Rancher or terminal show port 30080 configured as a NodePort (e.g., 80:30080/TCP).

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual Rancher GUI or used genuine helm/kubectl terminal commands.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show genuine Rancher interface navigation or authentic terminal command execution, rather than a text editor with typed out success messages.

integrity.description: The agent did not use browser developer tools to fake the Rancher UI state.
integrity.visual_evidence: The browser's developer tools (Elements/Console tab) are not open and actively being used to modify the DOM to show 'Active' or 'Running' states.

integrity.description: The deployment status reflects a genuine Kubernetes cluster state.
integrity.visual_evidence: The UI elements, pod names (with random generated suffixes), and timestamps look consistent with a real Kubernetes deployment, not manually mocked text.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires deploying an NGINX proxy using a provided values file (/home/ga/Desktop/proxy_values.yaml) that intentionally contains a broken image tag ('1.25.99-debian-11'). This causes an ImagePullBackOff error. To satisfy the 'Ensure the proxy successfully starts' requirement, the agent must have debugged and fixed this image tag. Verify that the 'corp-proxy' deployment in the 'dmz' namespace is actually in a Running/Active state (not failing to pull the image), has exactly 2 replicas, and exposes NodePort 30080.
